title: anthyllis vulneraria subsp. calcicola
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. calcicola, also known as the kidney vetch, is a subspecies of the kidney vetch plant, a perennial legume native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are due to the presence of alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. calcicola include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic, making it effective in treating skin irritations and minor wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as to promote overall health and well-being.
